title: A Lightweight Multiscale Neural Network for Inversion of NMR Relaxation Measurements in Porous Media
author: Luo, Gang
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Low-field nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) logging plays an important role in reservoir evaluation. However, low-field NMR signals are extremely weak with low signal-to-noise ratios (SNR), resulting in low resolution of NMR relaxation spectra and difficulties in quantitative evaluation of fluid components. In this paper, a lightweight multi-scale neural network (LMsNN) for relaxation spectrum inversion is proposed. The pulse sequence response equation is introduced to implement self-supervised training of the neural network, which adaptively extracts key features in the echo signal to invert the T 2 spectrum. A series of constraints are also added to reduce artefacts in the inverted spectrum and enhance the robustness of the optimization process. Validated by numerical synthesis, core analysis and NMR logging data processing, the LMsNN method works well for formations with different physical properties. The new method can help to accurately indicate the pore distribution and fluid components of different rock types.
